challenges.title: Carbon Offset
challenges.summary: A retreat photo looks normal until the bytes after the JPEG footer are inspected.
challenges.scenario: An employee under review sent one final image to the team chat before their laptop was seized. The picture itself is harmless, but the file structure suggests it was used as a dead-drop for a plaintext confession. Students should inspect the image footer, recover the appended payload, and extract the hidden flag.
learningObjectives: Recover appended evidence from a seemingly normal media file

artifacts.name: retreat_photo.jpg
artifacts.purpose: Primary image containing appended hidden data

artifacts.name: briefing.txt
artifacts.purpose: Short case note handed to solvers

hints: A JPEG should stop after its normal footer.
Check the tail of the file instead of only viewing the image.

llmHandoff.scope: Only this folder should be needed to build the final challenge bundle.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Create a believable JPEG and append either plaintext or lightly encoded text after the real FFD9 footer.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not change the portal UI or create cross-folder dependencies.

challenges.title: Ledger Burn
challenges.summary: A valid ZIP archive was padded with a hidden payload after its official end-of-directory record.
challenges.scenario: Finance exported a payroll archive right before a workstation wipe. The ZIP opens normally, but a forensic review shows the file extends well beyond the expected archive boundary. Students must identify where the archive truly ends, carve the appended payload, and recover the flag from the concealed object.
learningObjectives: Validate archive metadata against raw file length
Carve appended content after a legitimate container footer

artifacts.name: quarter_close.zip
artifacts.purpose: Primary archive with hidden data appended after EOCD

artifacts.name: audit_note.txt
artifacts.purpose: Case narrative that points to suspicious accounting activity

hints: Use archive metadata to find the real end of the ZIP.
The archive is not corrupt. Something was added after it.

llmHandoff.scope: Implement the challenge entirely inside this folder and keep the archive self-contained.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Create a clean ZIP, append a second file type after the EOCD, and document the exact carve offsets in the internal solution.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not add extra stego layers unless the team explicitly wants a higher difficulty.

challenges.title: Template Bleed
challenges.summary: A DOCX review packet leaks evidence through embedded media and leftover office package internals.
challenges.scenario: HR exported a disciplinary report as a DOCX and claimed the original evidence had been removed. A closer look at the Office container reveals that the package still holds media artifacts that were not meant to survive distribution. Students should unpack the document, inspect its internal structure, and recover the hidden flag from an embedded file.
learningObjectives: Treat Office files as ZIP containers during forensic review
Locate hidden or overlooked media inside application package structures

artifacts.name: performance_review.docx
artifacts.purpose: Primary Office document with hidden evidence in package contents

artifacts.name: case_summary.txt
artifacts.purpose: Background note framing the insider investigation

hints: A DOCX is not a single opaque file format.
Check the `word/` package contents before trying heavy carving.

llmHandoff.scope: Keep all challenge files and solution notes isolated to this folder.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Build a DOCX with plausible business content and hide the real clue in an embedded image or media object inside the package.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not turn this into a deleted-file recovery challenge unless the course scope changes.

challenges.title: Graveyard Transfer
challenges.summary: A late-night FTP session moved more than it should have, and the useful evidence is buried in the data stream.
challenges.scenario: A contractor claimed they only uploaded harmless logs during a night shift. The packet capture shows an FTP control session plus a short-lived high-numbered data connection that transferred a suspicious file. Students must reconstruct the transferred object from the capture, inspect the recovered payload, and identify the embedded flag.
learningObjectives: Map control traffic to a separate FTP data channel
Reconstruct transferred evidence from packet captures

artifacts.name: graveyard_transfer.pcap
artifacts.purpose: Primary network capture containing control and data streams

artifacts.name: incident_memo.txt
artifacts.purpose: Narrative note describing the suspicious transfer window

hints: The interesting bytes are probably not on port 21.
Recover the transferred file before trying to search for the flag.

llmHandoff.scope: This folder should contain the full PCAP-based challenge bundle and solution notes.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Generate a realistic FTP capture where the flag is only recoverable after reconstructing the transferred artifact from the data channel.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not require malware reversing beyond simple strings or metadata unless difficulty is intentionally raised.

challenges.title: MarketKey Replay
challenges.summary: An Android access-token generator must be decompiled to reproduce the suspect's login value for a specific date.
challenges.scenario: Investigators seized an APK used by a marketplace operator to generate rotating daily access tokens. The suspect insists the token on the target date cannot be reproduced without the original phone. Students must reverse the app logic, recover the secret material, and calculate the exact token that unlocks the final flag.
learningObjectives: Extract token-generation logic from a mobile application
Reproduce a date-driven credential outside the original runtime

artifacts.name: MarketKey.apk
artifacts.purpose: Primary application to decompile and analyze

artifacts.name: timeline.txt
artifacts.purpose: Case note with the date the token must match

hints: Search the decompiled code for date formatting and crypto primitives.
The final answer should come from recreating the token logic, not brute force.

llmHandoff.scope: All app samples, helper scripts, and solution notes should stay inside this folder.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Create or adapt an APK where the token algorithm can be recovered statically and replayed for one target date.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not add server-side validation unless the challenge is intentionally expanded into a live service.
